description: As part of join work between Atlas Axis and Core Facilitator teams, this pull request migrates the Atlas from HTML to Markdown. This allows us to express the full hierarchical structure of the Atlas and will support future automations and AI integrations that are under development.

diff: - The contract will confirm the relayer status of the operator. If the caller does not have the relayer role the transaction will revert.

- The contract will ensure the `Rate Limits` allow for transferring the specified amount of the asset to the destination. If the transfer amount does not fall within the available Rate Limit, the transaction will revert.
- The contract will execute the ERC-20 `transfer` function, sending the specified amount of the asset to the destination address.
description: @blimpa @Coolcatcatio This PR updates all lists within the Atlas to follow the markdown specification. It also adds a markdownlint config file to start the process of fixing formatting across editors.

diff: Only an operator with the
relayer
role is able to transfer ERC-20 assets. To do so, they must call the
`transferAsset`
function on the Controller contract on mainnet, providing the ERC20 asset address, the destination address, and the amount to transfer. Calling this function will carry out the following actions:
- The contract will confirm the relayer status of the operator. If the caller does not have the relayer role the transaction will revert.
- The contract will ensure the
`Rate Limits`
allow for transferring the specified amount of the asset to the destination. If the transfer amount does not fall within the available Rate Limit, the transaction will revert.
- The contract will execute the ERC-20
`transfer`
function, sending the specified amount of the asset to the destination address.
The function call is as follows:

prTitle: Atomic Atlas: decompose Sky Atlas.md into one-folder-per-document tree + sync tooling

diff: - The contract will confirm the relayer status of the operator. If the caller does not have the relayer role the transaction will revert.
- The contract will ensure the `
Rate Limits
RateLimits
` allow for transferring the specified amount of the asset to the destination. If the transfer amount does not fall within the available Rate Limit, the transaction will revert.
- The contract will execute the ERC-20 `transfer` function, sending the specified amount of the asset to the destination address.
